properties.content:
DigiCert Faces Challenges Amid Mass Certificate Revocations

DigiCert is in the process of revoking thousands of certificates due to a recently discovered domain validation issue. This incident, impacting 83,267 certificates and 6,807 subscribers, was communicated to customers on July 29. Adhering to CA/Browser Forum (CABF) rules, DigiCert must revoke the affected certificates within 24 hours of notification.

Some customers, particularly those in critical infrastructure, telecommunications, cloud services, and healthcare, have faced difficulties in reissuing certificates within the tight timeframe. “Many large organizations cannot reissue and deploy new certificates everywhere in time,” said Jeremy Rowley, DigiCert's CISO.

In response to the urgency, DigiCert has been coordinating with browser representatives and customers to delay revocations under exceptional circumstances to avoid critical service interruptions. However, the company stated that all impacted certificates would be revoked no later than August 3, 2024, at 19:30 UTC.

Encountered challenges with @SectigoHQ Code Signing Cert process. CSR issue discovered after validation stage forced us to restart all process, causing delays. A pre-validation CSR check could've saved us time. Tnx, #Sectigo support for help, they issued the cert as fast as can.
